name: Sugar Valley
description_short: Sugar Valley FCU is a member-owned federal credit union serving employees of Western Sugar Cooperative, regional schools, healthcare, and government agencies in Nebraska.
description_long:
Sugar Valley Federal Credit Union was formed on April 1st, 2011, through the merger of Scotts Bluff FCU and Sugar Workers FCU. This consolidation expanded the membership base and operational capacity of the combined institution, creating a stronger community-focused credit union serving the Scottsbluff, Nebraska area. The credit union is membership-based and not-for-profit, operating under federal charter (NCUA-insured).

Sugar Valley FCU offers a range of financial services including deposit accounts, loans, and online banking. Members can access competitive rates and variety of loan options. The credit union provides online banking services accessible via their website and maintains a physical office location in Scottsbluff. They actively promote membership growth through educational initiatives like scholarship programs, offering $250 scholarships to qualifying high school seniors continuing their education.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its community involvement and restricted membership model. Rather than serving the general public, Sugar Valley FCU focuses on specific employer groups and their families, including Western Sugar Cooperative, cities of Scottsbluff and Gering, county and school employees, healthcare workers at Regional West Medical Center and Mitchell Health Care Center, and state and federal government employees. This selective membership structure creates a tight-knit member community with shared professional interests.

Sugar Valley FCU is a legitimate, federally-insured credit union with established operations and volunteer governance. However, potential members must verify eligibility through one of their sponsored employer groups—general public membership is not available. The limited service information available on their website suggests a smaller, regional institution focused on local community needs rather than comprehensive financial services.
pros: Restricted membership creates community-focused member benefits
Offers competitive rates on loans and accounts as stated on website
Active in community through scholarship programs for local students
Online banking available for member convenience
Multiple contact methods including phone, email, and physical branch location
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns profits to members
cons: Membership is restricted to specific employer groups—not open to general public
Limited service details on website; specific loan products and rates not disclosed
Small regional credit union may have fewer branch locations and ATM access than larger institutions
Website lacks comprehensive information about accounts, fees, and loan terms
Actively seeking supervisory volunteers suggests possible operational capacity constraints
best_for: Employees of Western Sugar Cooperative and their families
Public employees in Scottsbluff, Gering, and Scottsbluff County
Healthcare workers at Regional West Medical Center and affiliated clinics
State of Nebraska and US Government employees living in the area
diagnosis: Sugar Valley FCU is best for employees of sponsoring organizations (Western Sugar Cooperative, regional schools, healthcare systems, and government agencies) seeking a community-focused credit union with competitive rates and member-oriented governance. The primary limitation is eligibility—membership is restricted to affiliated employee groups, making this institution unavailable to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
